summary: End-to-end testing of Projexino backend (FastAPI + Mongo) and frontend (React + R3F). 23/23 pytest backend tests pass covering auth, leads CRUD + notes + analytics, tasks CRUD, dashboard stats, and CORS. Frontend: landing page renders R3F hero (torus knot + orange ring), /services renders cards, /portal renders 2 phone 3D mockups, /login authenticates admin and redirects to /app/dashboard, dashboard shows stat cards + funnel, /app/leads shows all 5 kanban columns + working New Lead modal with required validation, /app/tasks shows 4 columns, logout redirects to /login.

minor.issue: Preflight is intercepted by Kubernetes ingress and returns 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *' WITHOUT 'Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true'. Browsers using withCredentials may fail preflight even though actual POST/GET responses correctly include allow-credentials:true. Mitigated in frontend by Bearer token fallback from localStorage (pj_token).

issues: Two 401 console errors on unauthenticated landing page from /api/auth/me probe — expected but noisy; could be silenced by suppressing axios error log for /auth/me 401

action_items: Add data-testid attributes to New Lead/New Task modal inputs (e.g. lead-name-input, lead-company-input, lead-create-submit, task-title-input, task-create-submit) and to the global search bar so Playwright targeting is unambiguous.
Consider silencing the initial /api/auth/me 401 on the public landing page (e.g., skip the call if no pj_token in localStorage) — reduces noisy console errors for visitors.

Drag-and-drop kanban reordering was not exercised end-to-end via Playwright (HTML5 DnD + @hello-pangea/dnd is non-trivial to simulate); status patch is verified via backend test. Suggest adding a simple 'Move to' status dropdown as an a11y/test affordance, or a dedicated e2e DnD test in a follow-up.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: bcrypt hashing OK ($2b$ format), httpOnly+Secure+SameSite=None cookie set correctly, admin seed updates password on mismatch — good. No brute-force lockout implemented (5+ failed login attempts not rate-limited) — recommend adding for production.
server.py: All Mongo queries correctly exclude `_id` via projection {'_id':0}. owner_id scoping applied to leads/tasks — correct.
server.py: CORS uses allow_origin_regex='https?://.*' with allow_credentials=True. FastAPI app-level CORS is fine, but ingress layer overrides preflight headers — see backend minor issue.
frontend/src/lib/api.js: withCredentials:true + Bearer header fallback is a good defense-in-depth pattern.
Frontend code structure not exhaustively reviewed (token budget); Hero3D + PhoneMockup3D render confirmed by canvas presence and screenshot.

success_rate.frontend: ~90% (all primary flows verified; DnD and full lead/task creation through UI not fully automated)

test_credentials: admin@projexino.com / Projexino@2026 (verified working). team@projexino.com / Team@2026 (seeded, not exercised).
seed_data_creation: Pytest creates and cleans up TEST_-prefixed leads/tasks within each test class. A random TEST_*@example.com user is created during register test and left in DB (acceptable test artifact).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression suite lives at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py — run with: pytest backend/tests/backend_test.py -v. Frontend uses Bearer fallback (localStorage pj_token), so even if cookies are blocked by ingress preflight, the app continues to work. When automating modals, target inputs within a dialog scope rather than via page-wide selectors because the top app bar's search input collides with generic input lookups. R3F renders correctly; do NOT wait for networkidle — use 'load' + fixed sleep.
rca of the issue: No app-breaking issues found. The CORS preflight ingress quirk is the only environmental concern and is already mitigated by the Bearer-token fallback in /app/frontend/src/lib/api.js.
